items.q: If one EM wave has a higher frequency than another, what is true about its wavelength?
items.correct: It is shorter — wavelength and frequency are inversely related (c = f x lambda).
items.misconception: It is also longer, because both increase together.
items.random_wrong: It is unchanged — only the speed differs.
items.why: Students treat all wave properties as moving together (more of one = more of the other), missing the fixed-speed constraint that ties them inversely.

items.q: Why are infrared cameras used to detect warm objects in the dark?
items.correct: Warm objects emit infrared radiation due to their temperature; the camera detects this emission.
items.misconception: Infrared cameras shine infrared light onto objects so they reflect back visibly.
items.random_wrong: Infrared cameras detect heat moving through the air around objects.
items.why: Students assume cameras always work by reflection (like visible-light cameras), missing that thermal cameras passively detect emitted IR.

items.q: Is ultraviolet light visible to humans?
items.correct: No — even though it sits next to violet on the spectrum, its frequency is above the range our eyes detect.
items.misconception: Yes — UV is visible because it's right next to violet.
items.random_wrong: Yes — UV is the colour purple.
items.why: Students assume adjacency on the EM spectrum implies visibility; they don't appreciate the sharp biological cut-off at around 400 nm.

items.q: Which EM wave has the LONGEST wavelength?
items.why: Correct answer: Radio waves. “Gamma rays” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which EM wave is used in TV remote controls?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Infrared. “Microwaves” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many distinct regions of the EM spectrum are there at GCSE level?
items.why: Exact answer: 7. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
